name: ALPR core schema: detections, encounters, watchlist, route_turns
summary: Core database schema for ALPR data plus a related route_turns table that the stalking heuristic consumes (route_turns is independently useful, e.g. for analytics, so it is intentionally not gated on alpr_enabled). All plate text columns are stored encrypted (BYTEA ciphertext) and indexed by HMAC hash; cleartext never touches the DB. This feature defines tables, indexes, and sqlc query files only -- no handlers, no workers, no business logic. Migrations are idempotent so they run safely even when the user never enables ALPR.
acceptance_criteria: New migration sql/migrations/NNNN_alpr_schema.up.sql + matching .down.sql. Use the next sequential number after the latest existing migration; check sql/migrations/ for the highest current number.

Table plate_detections: id BIGSERIAL PK, dongle_id TEXT NOT NULL, route TEXT NOT NULL, segment INT NOT NULL, frame_offset_ms INT NOT NULL, plate_ciphertext BYTEA, plate_hash BYTEA NOT NULL (32 bytes from HMAC-SHA256), bbox JSONB NOT NULL ({x,y,w,h} pixel coords), confidence REAL NOT NULL, ocr_corrected BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE, gps_lat DOUBLE PRECISION, gps_lng DOUBLE PRECISION, gps_heading_deg REAL, frame_ts TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, thumb_path TEXT NULL (relative path under storage), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(). Indexes: (dongle_id, route, frame_ts), (plate_hash, frame_ts DESC), (frame_ts) for retention sweeps.

Table plate_encounters: id BIGSERIAL PK, dongle_id TEXT NOT NULL, route TEXT NOT NULL, plate_hash BYTEA NOT NULL, first_seen_ts TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, last_seen_ts TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, detection_count INT NOT NULL, turn_count INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, max_internal_gap_seconds INT NOT NULL, signature_id BIGINT NULL, status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active' CHECK (status IN ('active','reviewed','whitelist','alerted')), bbox_first JSONB, bbox_last JSONB, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(), updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(). Unique constraint on (dongle_id, route, plate_hash, first_seen_ts) so re-aggregation is idempotent. Indexes: (plate_hash, last_seen_ts DESC), (dongle_id, route).

Table plate_watchlist: id BIGSERIAL PK, plate_hash BYTEA UNIQUE NOT NULL, label_ciphertext BYTEA NULL, kind TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (kind IN ('whitelist','alerted','note')), severity SMALLINT NULL CHECK (severity BETWEEN 1 AND 5), first_alert_at TIMESTAMPTZ NULL, last_alert_at TIMESTAMPTZ NULL, acked_at TIMESTAMPTZ NULL, notes TEXT NULL, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(), updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(). Index on (kind, last_alert_at DESC) for the alert feed.

Table plate_alert_events: id BIGSERIAL PK, plate_hash BYTEA NOT NULL, route TEXT NULL, dongle_id TEXT NULL, computed_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(), severity SMALLINT NOT NULL, components JSONB NOT NULL (list of {name, points, evidence}), heuristic_version TEXT NOT NULL. This is the audit trail for why an alert fired -- never deleted by retention.

Table route_turns: id BIGSERIAL PK, dongle_id TEXT NOT NULL, route TEXT NOT NULL, turn_ts TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL, turn_offset_ms INT NOT NULL, bearing_before_deg REAL NOT NULL, bearing_after_deg REAL NOT NULL, delta_deg REAL NOT NULL, gps_lat DOUBLE PRECISION, gps_lng DOUBLE PRECISION. Unique on (dongle_id, route, turn_offset_ms) for idempotent reprocessing. Index on (dongle_id, route, turn_ts).

Table alpr_segment_progress: (dongle_id, route, segment) PK, processed_at_extractor TIMESTAMPTZ NULL, processed_at_detector TIMESTAMPTZ NULL. Worker bookkeeping for which segments have been ALPR-processed; lives in this migration since it is purely schema.

Table alpr_audit_log: id BIGSERIAL PK, action TEXT NOT NULL (whitelist_add, whitelist_remove, plate_edit, plate_merge, ack, unack, tuning_change), actor TEXT NULL (user identifier from session), payload JSONB NOT NULL, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(). Used by watchlist API and tuning UI features.

sqlc query files in sql/queries/: alpr_detections.sql (InsertDetection, ListDetectionsForRoute, ListDetectionsForRouteSince, DeleteDetectionsOlderThan, DeleteDetectionsOlderThanForUnflagged, UpdateDetectionPlate, UpdateDetectionPlateHash for merge), alpr_encounters.sql (UpsertEncounter, DeleteEncountersForRoute, ListEncountersForRoute, ListEncountersForPlate, ListEncountersForPlateInWindow, ListEncountersForSignatureInArea), alpr_watchlist.sql (UpsertWatchlistAlerted, UpsertWatchlistWhitelist, RemoveWatchlist, GetWatchlistByHash, ListWatchlistByKind, AckWatchlist, UnackWatchlist, ListAlerts, CountUnackedAlerts, MaxOpenSeverity), alpr_alert_events.sql (InsertAlertEvent, ListEventsForPlate), route_turns.sql (DeleteTurnsForRoute, InsertTurns (batch), ListTurnsForRoute, CountTurnsInWindow), alpr_segment_progress.sql (MarkExtractorProcessed, MarkDetectorProcessed, ListUnprocessedSegments, IsExtractorProcessed), alpr_audit_log.sql (InsertAudit, ListAudit).
make sqlc regenerates without errors. Verify sql/queries/* sqlc tag names match the generated Go function names used by downstream features.
Down migration drops every table created here in reverse FK order.
Add a smoke check to ./.projd/scripts/smoke.sh (or new internal test) verifying the migration applies and rolls back cleanly on a fresh DB.
